title: Patient → person: Black/African American male with US Core race, ethnicity, birthsex
notes:
Derived from real fhir.patient e8b43f7b-55bb-23fc-ec97-4ff19ca70c3e (Synthea) and the exact cdm_ours_fhir.person row our pipeline produced for it.

The Patient__person edge emits exactly ONE person row.

Gender: stage-2 SQL prefers us-core-birthsex over Patient.gender (COALESCE(us_core_birthsex, gender)). Birthsex='M' resolves via cm.gender_to_omop → gender_concept_id=8507 (vocab.concept: 8507 'MALE', domain Gender, standard 'S'). gender_source_value copies the resolved input ('M'), and gender_source_concept_id=8507 because the cm.gender_to_omop row for source_code='M' carries source_concept_id=8507 (the uppercase HL7 v3 AdministrativeGender code 'M' is itself loaded in Athena's Gender vocabulary, unlike lowercase 'male' which yields 0).

Race: resolution priority is omop-race extension > US Core OMB ombCategory > Synthea Other-recovery > 0. No omop-race extension here, so US Core ombCategory '2054-5' resolves via cm.race_omb_to_omop → race_concept_id=8516 (vocab.concept: 8516 'Black or African American', domain Race, standard 'S'). race_source_value = the US Core race text 'Black or African American' (omop_race_display absent, falls back to race_text). race_source_concept_id=0 — OMB race codes are not loaded as concept_code under any Athena race vocabulary (OMOP Race uses integer codes), so the honest source-concept mapping is 0 per CLAUDE.md.

Ethnicity: same priority. No omop-ethnicity extension, so OMB '2186-5' resolves via cm.ethnicity_omb_to_omop → ethnicity_concept_id=38003564 (vocab.concept: 38003564 'Not Hispanic or Latino', domain Ethnicity, standard 'S'). ethnicity_source_value = 'Not Hispanic or Latino' (US Core text). ethnicity_source_concept_id=0 (OMB ethnicity codes not loaded as a non-standard ethnicity vocab).

Birth date: birthDate '1992-01-25' → year_of_birth=1992, month_of_birth=1, day_of_birth=25 via EXTRACT. birth_datetime falls back to birthDate at midnight ('1992-01-25T00:00:00') because Synthea emits no patient-birthTime extension on the birthDate element.

person_source_value = Patient.id (the FHIR resource UUID), a traceability anchor — NOT the SS/MR identifiers in Patient.identifier[] (those are PHI and excluded by design).

location_id: NON-NULL. It is NOT hash(Patient.id); it is stringToId(concat_ws('|', line, city, state, zip)) = a hash of the address composite '564 Connelly Esplanade|Chelmsford|MA|00000'. Two patients at the same address share this key, per OMOP LOCATION ETL convention; Patient__location.sql writes the matching cdm.location row. It is represented here symbolically as ref:patient-1-location (the patient's own address-derived location), since there is no separate FHIR Location resource.

provider_id and care_site_id are NULL — this Patient has no generalPractitioner and no managingOrganization, so referenceToId(NULL) yields NULL.

deceasedDateTime is present on the source but the OMOP person table has no death column (death is a separate table outside this edge), so it does not affect the person row; kept on the input as faithful context but read by no person field.
